steps.title: Set the equations equal
steps.content:
Both equations give y, so equate the right‑hand sides: $$-\frac{1}{4}x = \frac{1}{6}x$$.
This means the only possible x must satisfy this equality.

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Move all terms to one side: $$-\frac{1}{4}x - \frac{1}{6}x = 0$$.
Factor x: $$x\left(-\frac{1}{4}-\frac{1}{6}\right)=0$$.
Compute the bracket: $$-\frac{3}{12}-\frac{2}{12} = -\frac{5}{12}$$.
Thus $$-\frac{5}{12}x = 0$$ and dividing by $$-\frac{5}{12}$$ yields $$x = 0$$.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
